few_shot_examples.answer:
An autonomous research improvement loop, also referred to as autoresearch, is a self-improving pattern for research. It involves a cycle of profiling, editing, benchmarking, keeping or reverting, to iterate towards better results [1].

This pattern has been applied to various domains, including genealogy (mattprusak/autoresearch-genealogy), voice AI agents (ArchishmanSengupta/autovoiceevals), trading agents (chrisworsey55/atlas-gic), and GPU kernel optimization (RightNow-AI/autokernel) [2].

Autoresearch has also been extended through the development of general-purpose descendants, such as WecoAI/aideml, which implements a two-loop architecture for iterative optimization and synthesis [3].

Datadog provides comprehensive monitoring and analytics solutions for companies. It offers real-time monitoring of servers, databases, tools, and services across IT infrastructure, helping companies ensure their applications and services run smoothly. Datadog offers unified monitoring, application performance monitoring (APM), log management, and infrastructure observability, using a usage-based pricing model [3].

few_shot_examples.query: how to scale an agentic system?
few_shot_examples.answer:
Scaling an agentic system requires careful consideration of several factors, including model deployment, model monitoring and optimization, agent memory, scalability, and data privacy governance.

According to [7] and [9], scalability can be achieved by adding new worker agents and domains seamlessly while keeping performance strong. Deploying at scale under tighter resource budgets is also crucial.

The A.G.E.N.T. framework provides a practical guide to launch and scale agentic AI, which includes auditing workflows, prototyping fast, and tracking value ([10]).

It's essential to have specific domain-oriented agents that can perform domain-specific tasks and ensure data privacy and governance ([9]).

There is no one-size-fits-all approach to scaling an agentic system, as it depends on the specific use case and requirements. However, by considering these factors and using a scalable deployment strategy, organizations can successfully scale their agentic systems.

References:

[7] This paper shows how to scale agentic software to be much larger ...
[8] How to Scale Agentic AI: A McKinsey & Synera Framework | Synera
[9] Scaling Agentic AI: Strategy for Enterprise-Wide Implementation
[10] How to Start and Scale Agentic AI: A.G.E.N.T. + 6-Step Strategy
